author: dunkin-novice
goal: Stop the daily board and reading page from blocking on the 4.6MB cards.json on cold loads (BUG-021).
done: Diagnosed via headless Chrome that both loadTarotManifest (board) and loadTarotData (reading) blocked on /data/cards.json (4.6MB raw / 1.15MB brotli) — board stayed blank until it downloaded, reading sat on 'Please wait a moment…'. Fix: (1) new scripts/generate-cards-manifest.mjs emits data/cards-manifest.json (~28KB raw / ~2-3KB compressed: id/names/image/slug only); loadTarotManifest fetches the manifest first with graceful fallback to the full cards.json if missing/shape-invalid. (2) renderDaily background-prefetches loadTarotData() via requestIdleCallback after the board is ready, warming HTTP+localStorage so the reading loads from cache. (3) generate-seo.yml regenerates the manifest on every push so it can't drift from cards.json.
root_cause: loadTarotManifest fetched the full 4.6MB cards.json then minimalised client-side, so the selection board (which only needs card identity) waited on 1.15MB; the reading page re-fetched the same file cold after navigation.
fix: Board-only manifest (manifest-first fetch + fallback) + background prefetch of the full deck during card selection + CI regeneration of the manifest.
verified: headless Chrome: board populated 571ms on localhost (fix) vs 3450ms on prod; reading page no longer re-fetches cards.json (served from prefetch-warmed cache)
md5 canonical==www==ios for data.js, main.js, cards-manifest.json
manifest generator output: 156 cards, 28,519 bytes raw
notes: Background prefetch is a cache-warm, not a payload cut — the full cards.json is still 4.6MB raw, so a reading reached without the prefetch (deep link, fast tap) is still slow; a real per-language/per-card split is a follow-up. Minor finds during diagnosis: tapping a card during the board's post-data re-render can miss (selection not sticking); LocalNotifications.then() throws an uncaught CapacitorException on web (pre-existing). BUG-021 recorded with these follow-ups.
